competitive_position.summary: Zycus is one of four vendors in a formal RFI process (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua) competing for a $400k, full-suite S2P deal at United Overseas Bank Limited Co. Rep confidence is 50% post-presentation (25 Jun), signaling perceived competitive parity. No competitive intelligence from buyer calls (zero Avoma calls discovered). SAP Ariba has ERP integration advantage if UOB runs SAP; Coupa/GEP have aggressive ROI positioning; iValua competes on configurability. Zycus differentiation is undocumented in Salesforce and untested in stakeholder conversations. Shortlist decision pending since 26 Jun; no feedback received as of 30 Jun. Competitive risk is high without a champion, a quantified ROI story, or confirmed AI/Merlin positioning as a decision criterion.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Four credible enterprise S2P competitors in tight RFI race; SAP Ariba has ERP integration advantage (prior sweep competitive intel)
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin AI-powered negotiation and intelligent automation as the wedge against Ariba's legacy workflow-driven platform; demonstrate faster cycle time and lower cost-per-PO vs SAP's complex multi-module licensing; secure Economic Buyer sponsorship to override IT's ERP-integration bias.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Coupa has aggressive ROI positioning (prior sweep competitive intel)
competitors.how_we_win: Counter Coupa's fast-ROI narrative by co-building a three-year ROI model with UOB anchored to quantified pain (manual errors, cycle time, spend visibility); show deeper S2P integration (iContract + iSource + iSupplier as unified suite vs Coupa's bolt-on modules) and superior AI capability (Merlin vs Coupa's basic ML).

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: GEP has aggressive ROI positioning (prior sweep competitive intel)
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on AI/Merlin agentic negotiation depth vs GEP's consulting-led approach; demonstrate SaaS maturity and regional Singapore support vs GEP's heavier consulting dependency; anchor to UOB's decision criteria if AI is a scored item.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: iValua competes on configurability and supplier collaboration (prior sweep competitive intel)
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the AI-first platform (Merlin) vs iValua's configuration-heavy, traditional S2P; show faster time-to-value with SaaS delivery vs iValua's on-prem legacy; emphasize procurement-led vs IT-led implementation to appeal to Gillian's procurement-process ownership.

champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (Gillian Chua is process owner, not advocate); Next_Step__c (rep 50% confidence); Avoma manifest (Shiwei Liang role unknown)
champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Gillian Chua Kee Liang is a neutral process owner running a four-vendor RFI; she evaluates all vendors equally and does not provide internal coaching or competitor intelligence. Shiwei Liang's role and disposition are unknown. In a competitive RFI race (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, iValua), the lack of a champion leaves Zycus blind to internal decision dynamics, evaluation criteria weighting, and Economic Buyer priorities. The rep's 50% confidence (Next_Step__c) reflects this gap. Without a champion who shares the scorecard, warns of competitor moves, and connects Zycus to the EB, Zycus is at a significant disadvantage.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Gillian Chua Kee Liang (Head of Strategic Sourcing, process owner, not EB)
25 Jun presentation attendee list includes Shiwei Liang (role unknown)
Next_Step__c: no EB or C-level engagement mentioned
Account contact roster includes CIO (Varun Rally), transformation leads (Daniel Zammit, Clement Ang, Atul Bhise), but none confirmed engaged on this opp
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. In a $400k enterprise S2P initiative at a Tier-1 APAC bank, the CFO, CIO, or a senior procurement/transformation executive typically controls budget approval and vendor selection. The only confirmed buyer-side contact role is Gillian Chua Kee Liang (Executive Director, Head of Strategic Sourcing and Procurement), a process owner, not a budget authority. Shiwei Liang appears on the 25 Jun presentation invite but role/authority unknown. The Next_Step__c describes a procurement-led RFI process with no mention of C-level sponsorship, CFO sign-off, or executive steering. In a competitive four-vendor race (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, iValua), the vendor that maps and influences the EB first wins. The gap is a real risk: without EB access, Zycus cannot validate budget, timing, or decision authority, and a competitor with C-level sponsorship will control the outcome. The immediate move is to request an executive briefing for the CFO or CIO to validate the investment case and secure senior-level support.

recommended_moves: Secure shortlist decision and debrief 25 Jun presentation to extract evaluation scorecard and competitor intel.
Request executive briefing for Economic Buyer to validate investment case and secure C-level sponsorship.
Deploy AI/Merlin differentiation proof point via tailored demo or banking peer reference call.
